content:
## Decision

Detect collisions by **repo-family + intra-repo path**, never by raw dir string or content. Tag every root with a `familyId` derived from the worktree list discovery *already* computes (zero new git calls); a set's logical identity is `(familyId, pathRelativeToRoot)`. A name is a true collision iff its records span **>1 distinct identity**. On collision, keep today's single precedence-winner row (slug stays unique, Explorer never blanks) and annotate it with an optional `duplicateNameError`; also return a `collisions[]` diagnostics list and emit one deduped `console.error`. `readAllSessionSets()` keeps its `SessionSet[]` signature by delegating to a new `тАжWithDiagnostics()`, so the untouched view-model stays pixel-identical when nothing collides. **No throwing, no notifications from the data layer.**

---

### A. Collision predicate

**Rule**
- In discovery, tag every root with `familyId`. For each workspace folder `WF`: `familyId = realpath(mainWorktree)`, where `mainWorktree` is the **first** entry of `listGitWorktrees(WF)` (git always lists the main working tree first); assign that same `familyId` to `WF` and to each worktree it enumerates. Non-git folder тЖТ `familyId = realpath(WF)`. This reuses the enumeration discovery already runs тЖТ **no new git calls**.
- For each set: `relPath = toPosix(path.relative(root.dir, set.dir))` тАФ Phase 1 `docs/session-sets/<name>`, Phase 3 `docs/session-sets/<module>/<name>`.
- `identityKey(set) = familyId + "\u0000" + relPath`.
- Group all discovered records by `name`. **A name is a true collision iff it contains тЙе2 distinct `identityKey`s.** Same key across roots = the legitimate main+worktree copies тЖТ merge as today.

**Justification** Family grouping is the exact inverse of how roots were discovered, so worktree copies of one repo share a `familyId` by construction and never false-positive; two different repos in a multi-root workspace get different `familyId`s and are correctly flagged. Folding `relPath` into the key makes Phase 3's `<module>/<name>` nesting register as a collision with **zero predicate changes** (only `readSessionSets` populates a deeper relPath). Content hashing rejected (worktree copies legitimately diverge mid-session); "any differing dir" rejected (worktree copies *always* differ by root prefix тЖТ would flag every legitimate merge); git-common-dir is the canonical equivalent but costs a call per root, so we approximate it for free via the main-worktree path.

### B. Surfacing

**Session 1 ships (data layer only):**
1. Optional field `SessionSet.duplicateNameError?: DuplicateNameError` on the winning row. Undefined for every non-collided set тЖТ view-model ignores it тЖТ today's rendering is byte-identical. Inert until Session 2 draws it.
2. `readAllSessionSetsWithDiagnostics(): { sets: SessionSet[]; collisions: DuplicateNameCollision[] }`; `readAllSessionSets()` keeps its signature via `return readAllSessionSetsWithDiagnostics().sets`. No mutable module state тАФ the collision list is a first-class return value.
3. One deduped `console.error` per distinct collision signature (`name` + sorted conflicting dirs), tracked in a module-level `Set<string>` reconciled each run (log on first appearance; re-arm when it clears). This is the "loud" artifact available *before* Session 2 exists.

```ts
export interface DuplicateNameError {
  name: string;
  chosenDir: string;          // == winning SessionSet.dir
  conflictingDirs: string[];  // all distinct identity dirs, sorted, incl. winner
}
export interface DuplicateNameCollision extends DuplicateNameError {
  candidates: Array<{ dir: string; familyId: string; state: SessionSetState; lastTouched?: string }>;
}
```

**Rejected:** throwing (discovery re-runs on every refresh тЖТ blanks the whole Explorer for one clash, violating the hard gate); `vscode.window.showErrorMessage` from the data layer (fires on every refresh/file-watch тЖТ spam; UI throttling is Session 2's job).

**Session 2 is expected to render:** an error affordance on the flagged row (badge + tooltip listing `conflictingDirs`, optionally disabling name-keyed actions on it) driven by `duplicateNameError`; and, from `collisions[]`, at most one throttled notification/status item. Session 2 owns all UI/protocol; the contract above is stable.

### C. Merge behavior under collision

**Rule** Return exactly **one row** for the collided name тАФ the same precedence winner today's merge already picks (state rank `complete>in-progress>not-started>cancelled`, tie-broken by `lastTouched`) тАФ with `duplicateNameError` set. Do **not** emit both copies; do **not** drop the name.

**Justification** `RowPayload.slug` and `findSetBySlug` are name-keyed, so two rows sharing a name/slug would misroute every action тАФ "both copies" is unsafe until Session 2 reworks slugs (out of scope). Dropping the name ("neither") deletes visible work, breaks prereq resolution that references it, and leaves nowhere to attach the error. Keeping the deterministic winner preserves slug uniqueness, keeps the Explorer populated, and тАФ because the row is now flagged тАФ converts today's *silent* misroute into a *loud, actionable* error. Full ambiguity is preserved for the operator in `conflictingDirs`/`collisions[]`.

---

### Test matrix

Stub `discoverRoots`/`listGitWorktrees`/`readSessionSets` per case.

| # | Setup | Expected |
|---|-------|----------|
| 1 | Single repo, no worktrees, unique names | `collisions == []`, no `duplicateNameError`, `readAllSessionSets()` === today's output, no `console.error` тАФ **backward-compat gate** |
| 2 | Main + 2 worktrees, same name in all three (same relPath, same family) | Exactly one merged row, winner = highest precedence, no error, no log тАФ **legitimate merge preserved** |
| 3 | Multi-root: repo A + repo B both expose `001-bootstrap` (distinct families) | One row; `duplicateNameError.conflictingDirs` = both dirs sorted; `collisions.length === 1`; `console.error` called once тАФ **case (a) fail-loud** |
| 4 | As #3 with A=complete, B=in-progress; then flip states/`lastTouched` | Winner = A, `chosenDir === A.dir`; asserts rank then tiebreak тАФ **deterministic winner == today's precedence** |
| 5 | Repos A,B collide on `001` but each also has unique `002`,`003` | Uniques present and error-free; only `001` flagged; nothing blanked тАФ **never blank Explorer** |
| 6 | Two consecutive discovery runs, persistent collision; then remove; then reintroduce | `console.error` called once total; no logs after removal; logs again on reintroduction тАФ **dedup / no spam** |
| 7 | Phase 3 layout: single family, `docs/session-sets/mod-a/001` + `docs/session-sets/mod-b/001` (same familyId, different relPath) | Flagged as collision тАФ **predicate is layout-agnostic** |
| 8 | Main checkout AND one of its worktrees BOTH added as workspace folders, same name | Same `familyId` for both тЖТ NOT a collision, one merged row, no error тАФ **family identity robust, no false positive** |
